events.title: US and Iran Agree Deal to End War, Reopen Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: The United States and Iran have reached an agreement to end their ongoing war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. The deal, described as an interim memorandum of understanding, includes an immediate and permanent termination of military operations on all fronts, including in Lebanon. This development has brought significant relief to global markets, with oil prices falling and shares rising.

events.title: Israeli Military Strikes Hezbollah Targets in Beirut
events.desc: The Israeli military announced it has struck targets belonging to the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah in Beirut. This action comes as Iran warns that such strikes could derail the recently announced US-Iran deal to end the broader conflict. The strikes are part of the ongoing escalation between Israel and Hezbollah along the Lebanese border.

events.title: Israeli Air Strikes Hit Southern Lebanon Amidst Tensions
events.desc: Reports indicate that Israeli air strikes have targeted southern Lebanon, with at least 17 casualties reported, including nine in the town of Tayr Debba. These strikes occur despite Iran's warnings to Israel to cease attacks on its ally Hezbollah, raising concerns about the stability of the new US-Iran peace deal.

events.title: Journalist Injured in Israeli Strike While Reporting in Lebanon
events.desc: A journalist working for Press TV was injured by shrapnel while covering Israeli attacks in southern Lebanon. The incident highlights the dangers faced by media personnel operating in conflict zones amidst escalating tensions between Israel and Hezbollah.

events.title: US Air Force B-52 Crashes in California
events.desc: A United States Air Force B-52 Stratofortress aircraft crashed shortly after takeoff in California. Eight individuals are believed to have died in the incident. The cause of the crash is under investigation.

events.title: Syria Reports Sabotage of Undersea Internet Cable
events.desc: Syria has reported the sabotage of an undersea internet cable connecting Tartous to Alexandria, Egypt. This cable is one of four that link to the coastal city of Tartous. The incident could impact internet connectivity and communication in the region.

events.title: South Sudan: Government and Opposition Accused of Atrocities in Jonglei
events.desc: Government and opposition forces in South Sudan's Jonglei region are accused of widespread destruction, including burning homes and disabling hospitals. This follows reports of burned villages and mass displacement, raising concerns about a humanitarian crisis and ongoing conflict.

events.title: Ukraine Opens EU Membership Talks
events.desc: Ukraine has officially commenced the first phase of its membership negotiations with the European Union. This marks a significant step in Ukraine's efforts to integrate with European political and economic structures.

events.title: Three Indian Sailors Killed in US Strike on Oil Tanker
events.desc: Three Indian sailors were killed when a US military strike hit a Palau-flagged oil tanker. The US stated the strike was carried out after the tanker failed to comply with directions. The incident raises tensions in maritime security operations.

events.title: Netanyahu States Israel Will Remain in Lebanon, Syria, and Gaza
events.desc: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has declared that Israeli forces will maintain a 'security zone' in occupied Lebanon, and will continue to operate in Syria and Gaza. This statement signals Israel's intention to continue its military presence and operations in these regions, potentially complicating regional stability.

events.title: Iran Draws 2-2 with New Zealand in World Cup Match Amidst Political Divisions
events.desc: Iran drew 2-2 with New Zealand in a politically charged World Cup match. The game highlighted deep divisions within the Iranian diaspora and among supporters, with protests against the Iranian government occurring outside the stadium. The team's performance was overshadowed by political allegiances.

events.title: Satellite Imagery Confirms Widespread Demolitions in Southern Lebanon
events.desc: Despite a fragile ceasefire, satellite imagery reveals extensive destruction in southern Lebanon. At least 46 out of 54 towns and villages within the Israeli military's 'Yellow Line' have been heavily damaged or completely flattened, indicating significant ongoing demolition and destruction.

events.title: Reports of Banned Russian Submunitions Found in Mali
events.desc: Unexploded Russian-made cluster munition bomblets, along with damage consistent with their impact, have been discovered in a village in northern Mali. This finding is significant as Mali is a state party to the convention banning such weapons.

events.title: US Firm HKN Energy Takes Over 7 Oil Fields in Syria
events.desc: US firm HKN Energy has assumed operational control of seven oil fields in Syria under a 25-year agreement with the Syrian government. This marks the first major production-sharing deal between the Syrian government and an international oil company since the conflict began.

events.title: Iranian Police Infrastructure Targeted Amidst 'Make Iran Ungovernable' Efforts
events.desc: Bellingcat has identified at least 80 police stations or infrastructure related to law enforcement and the Basij paramilitary group that have been damaged or destroyed in the first three weeks of the US and Israeli war against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's 'repressive capacity'.
